title: Jessie Buckley Wins Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Drama
text: Jessie Buckley claimed the Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Drama at the 83rd Annual Golden Globes on January 11, 2026, for her role in Hamnet. This victory marks a significant achievement for Buckley, extending her award-winning streak. She previously won the Critics Choice Award for the same performance and received a Screen Actors Guild nomination. Buckley competed against Jennifer Lawrence, Renate Reinsve, Julia Roberts, Tessa Thompson, and Eva Victor. This represented her inaugural Golden Globe nomination. During her acceptance remarks, Buckley expressed her gratitude to the Golden Globes and those involved with the film. She made a memorable acknowledgment of the film's key grip, Tomasz Sternicki, sharing an anecdote about his culinary contributions on set. She recounted finding him at the back of his truck chopping up potatoes and onions and meat, preparing soup in his cast iron pot brought from Poland. The homemade soup subsequently appeared regularly on the production. In this historical fiction film directed by Oscar winner Chloé Zhao, Buckley portrays Agnes Shakespeare opposite Paul Mescal as William Shakespeare. The narrative explores the playwright's love story and family tragedy following their son's death from plague. The film secured six Golden Globe nominations overall, winning both Best Drama and Buckley's acting award.
